text: People should stick closely to traditional values.
Respect for authority is one of the most important virtues.
Society works best when rules are enforced firmly.
Too much openness to new lifestyles weakens the social order.
Disobedient people usually deserve strong consequences.
Some people need harsh discipline to learn their place.
Troublemakers are best handled forcefully.
Mercy is overrated when order is at stake.
Real leaders must project strength and toughness.
Most conflicts are settled by power, not kindness.
People naturally sort into strong and weak.
A society without hard leadership falls apart.
Too much introspection makes people soft.
Feelings should not get in the way of duty.
People should toughen up instead of dwelling on emotions.
The world is full of hidden threats most people ignore.
Dangerous groups often work behind the scenes.
You can never be too careful about corruption spreading.
People are often plotting more than they admit.
Moral decline usually starts with permissiveness.
Sexual permissiveness is a sign of social decay.
Breaking family norms leads to disorder.
Clean-cut social roles keep society stable.
Strange beliefs and unusual lifestyles often signal deeper problems.
Ordinary people need guidance from stronger authorities.
People who challenge tradition too much often become destructive.
Softness invites chaos.
Hierarchy is natural and necessary.
Suspicion keeps you safe in a dangerous world.
Firm punishment is often the only language bad actors understand.
